texto: Mr. Jamrach led me through the lobby and into the menagerie. The first was a parrot room, a fearsome screaming place of mad round eyes, crimson breasts that beat against bars, wings that flapped against their neighbours, blood red, royal blue, gypsy yellow, grass green. The birds were crammed along perches. Macaws hung upside down here and there, batting their white eyes, and small green parrots flittered above our heads in drifts. A hot of cockatoos looked down from on high over the shrill madness, high crested, creamy breasted. The screeching was like laughter in hell.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carol Birch: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Mr. Jamrach led me through the lobby and into the menagerie. The first was a parrot room, a fears…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, animals, birds pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de birds e hell.

The first was a parrot room, a fearsome screaming place of mad round eyes, crimson breasts that beat against bars, wings that flapped against their neighbours, blood red, royal blue, gypsy yellow, grass green. The birds were crammed along perches. Macaws hung upside down here and there, batting their white eyes, and small green parrots flittered above our heads in drifts. A hot of cockatoos looked down from on high over the shrill madness, high crested, creamy breasted. The screeching was like laughter in hell.

texto: It was the first smile of my life. Of course, that is a ridiculous thing to say; I had been smiled at often, the big man had smiled at me not a minute since. And yet I say: it was the first smile, because it was the first that ever went straight into me like a needle too thin to be seen.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carol Birch: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “It was the first smile of my life. frase_original: It was the first smile of my life. Of course, that is a ridiculous thing to say; I had been smiled at often, the big man had smiled at me not a minute since. And yet I say: it was the first smile, because it was the first that ever went straight into me like a needle too thin to be seen.

texto: It was then I truly realised the whale is no more a fish than I am. So much blood. This was not like the fish on the quay, fresh caught, lying flipping and flopping, death on a simmer. This was a fierce, boiling death. She died thrashing blindly in a slick of gore, full of pain and fury, gnashing her jaws, beating her tail, spewing lumps of slime and half-digested fish that fell stinking about us. It was vile. So much strength dies slowly.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Carol Birch: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. texto: It’s one of the things I love about the sea, the way you can see weather afar. It’s like looking at the future.
